sections.title: Test coverage: `apps/crawler` (Python)
sections.content: **Current state: zero tests.** `pytest` is a dev dependency (`pyproject.toml`) but no `tests/` dir exists, no test files anywhere. Every function below is currently only exercised by running the crawler against live sites.

sections.title: base.py — pure logic, cheap to test, currently 100% untested
sections.content:
**`parse_french_date`** / **`parse_numeric_date`** — regex + dict lookup, no try/except around `datetime(...)` construction (invalid day/month int like `32` or `13` raises uncaught `ValueError`).

```python

sections.title: tests/test_base.py
sections.content:
import pytest
from base import parse_french_date, parse_numeric_date

@pytest.mark.parametrize("text,expected", [
    ("22 juin 2019", "2019-06-22"),
    ("octobre 30, 2017", "2017-10-30"),
    ("1 février 2020", "2020-02-01"),   # accented month
    ("1 fevrier 2020", "2020-02-01"),   # unaccented month
    ("no date here", None),
    ("22 blorpuary 2019", None),        # unknown month name
])
def test_parse_french_date(text, expected):
    assert parse_french_date(text) == expected

def test_parse_numeric_date_with_trailing_time():
    assert parse_numeric_date("13/07/2026 10:30") == "2026-07-13"

def test_parse_numeric_date_invalid_no_match():
    assert parse_numeric_date("not a date") is None

def test_parse_french_date_invalid_day_raises():
    # ponytail-gap: no bounds check before datetime() — documents current (crashing) behavior
    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        parse_french_date("32 juin 2019")
```

**`discover_sitemap_urls`** — sitemapindex recursion, namespace-stripped `loc` matching, and the deliberate exclusion of nested `image:image/image:loc` are the load-bearing logic here; none of it is tested.

```python
from unittest.mock import patch
from base import discover_sitemap_urls

SITEMAP_INDEX = b"""<?xml version="1.0"?>
<sitemapindex xmlns="http://www.sitemaps.org/schemas/sitemap/0.9">
  <sitemap><loc>https://x/sub1.xml</loc></sitemap>
</sitemapindex>"""

URLSET_WITH_IMAGE = b"""<?xml version="1.0"?>
<urlset xmlns="http://www.sitemaps.org/schemas/sitemap/0.9"
        xmlns:image="http://www.google.com/schemas/sitemap-image/1.1">
  <url>
    <loc>https://x/article-1</loc>
    <image:image><image:loc>https://x/photo.jpg</image:loc></image:image>
  </url>
</urlset>"""

def test_discover_sitemap_urls_recurses_index_and_skips_nested_image_loc():
    responses = iter([SITEMAP_INDEX, URLSET_WITH_IMAGE])
    with patch("base._fetch_xml", side_effect=lambda url: responses.__next__()):
        urls = list(discover_sitemap_urls("https://x/index.xml"))
    assert urls == ["https://x/article-1"]  # photo.jpg must NOT appear
```

**`_fetch_xml`** — gzip magic-byte sniffing regardless of headers, and stripping a stray leading newline. Both are provider-specific workarounds with a comment explaining *why*, but no regression test guarding them.

```python
import gzip
from unittest.mock import Mock, patch
from base import _fetch_xml

def test_fetch_xml_decompresses_gzip_even_without_header(monkeypatch):
    body = gzip.compress(b"<xml/>")
    resp = Mock()
    resp.read.return_value = body
    resp.__enter__ = Mock(return_value=resp)
    resp.__exit__ = Mock(return_value=False)
    with patch("urllib.request.urlopen", return_value=resp):
        assert _fetch_xml("https://x") == "<xml/>"

def test_fetch_xml_strips_leading_newline():
    resp = Mock()
    resp.read.return_value = b"\n<xml/>"
    resp.__enter__ = Mock(return_value=resp)
    resp.__exit__ = Mock(return_value=False)
    with patch("urllib.request.urlopen", return_value=resp):
        assert _fetch_xml("https://x") == "<xml/>"
```

**`NewsSource._load_state` / `_save_state`** — atomic write (tmp file + `replace`), missing-file default. Currently untested; a broken atomic-write would silently corrupt resumability.

```python
from base import NewsSource
from models import SiteSelectors

class _Dummy(NewsSource):
    source_name = "dummy-test"
    selectors = SiteSelectors(title="h1", body="p")

def test_load_state_defaults_when_missing(tmp_path, monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr("base.STATE_DIR", tmp_path)
    src = _Dummy()
    assert src._load_state() == {"seen_urls": [], "total_saved": 0}

def test_save_state_is_atomic_and_roundtrips(tmp_path, monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr("base.STATE_DIR", tmp_path)
    src = _Dummy()
    src._save_state({"seen_urls": ["a"], "total_saved": 1})
    assert not (tmp_path / "dummy-test.json.tmp").exists()  # tmp cleaned up via replace()
    assert src._load_state() == {"seen_urls": ["a"], "total_saved": 1}
```

**`NewsSource.parse_article`** — the biggest untested surface: title-missing → `None` short-circuit, `og:image` extraction, iframe-vs-`<video>` embed priority (and skipping `about:blank`), and each optional selector (`author`, `published_date` with `_attr` vs text+`format`, `category`) branching. This needs a fake `page` object (scrapling's `.css()`/`.first`/`.attrib`/`get_all_text` interface) — a small hand-rolled fake is the lazy option over pulling in a real fetcher.

```python
class FakeEl:
    def __init__(self, text="", attrib=None):
        self._text, self.attrib = text, attrib or {}
    def get_all_text(self, strip=True): return self._text

class FakePage:
    def __init__(self, by_selector): self._by = by_selector
    def css(self, selector):
        return self._by.get(selector, [])

def test_parse_article_returns_none_when_title_missing(monkeypatch):
    src = _Dummy()
    page = FakePage({"h1": []})
    monkeypatch.setattr(src, "fetch", lambda url: page)
    assert src.parse_article("https://x/a") is None

def test_parse_article_prefers_iframe_over_video_embed(monkeypatch):
    src = _Dummy()
    page = FakePage({
        "h1": [FakeEl("Title")],
        "p": [],
        "meta[property='og:image']": [],
        "iframe": [FakeEl(attrib={"src": "https://yt/embed"})],
        "video source": [FakeEl(attrib={"src": "https://x/video.mp4"})],
    })
    monkeypatch.setattr(src, "fetch", lambda url: page)
    monkeypatch.setattr(src, "_extract_images", lambda *a: [])
    article = src.parse_article("https://x/a")
    assert article.embed_url == "https://yt/embed"

def test_parse_article_skips_about_blank_iframe(monkeypatch):
    src = _Dummy()
    page = FakePage({
        "h1": [FakeEl("Title")], "p": [],
        "meta[property='og:image']": [],
        "iframe": [FakeEl(attrib={"src": "about:blank"})],
        "video source": [FakeEl(attrib={"src": "https://x/v.mp4"})],
    })
    monkeypatch.setattr(src, "fetch", lambda url: page)
    monkeypatch.setattr(src, "_extract_images", lambda *a: [])
    assert src.parse_article("https://x/a").embed_url == "https://x/v.mp4"
```

**`_extract_images`** — `data-src` over `src` priority, `data:` URI skip, dedup, cover-image-first ordering.

```python
def test_extract_images_prefers_data_src_dedups_and_skips_data_uri():
    src = _Dummy()
    page = FakePage({
        "article img, .entry-content img, .td-post-content img, "
        ".post-content img, .vcex-post-content img, figure img": [
            FakeEl(attrib={"src": "placeholder.png", "data-src": "/real.jpg"}),
            FakeEl(attrib={"src": "data:image/png;base64,xx"}),
            FakeEl(attrib={"src": "/real.jpg"}),  # duplicate after urljoin
        ]
    })
    images = src._extract_images(page, "https://x/a", cover_image="https://x/cover.jpg")
    assert images == ["https://x/cover.jpg", "https://x/real.jpg"]
```

**`crawl()`** — the core resumability state machine. This is the highest-value gap: a regression here silently breaks resumability or drops/duplicates articles across every provider. None of these branches are tested:

```python
def test_crawl_skips_already_seen_urls(monkeypatch):
    src = _Dummy()
    monkeypatch.setattr(src, "_load_state", lambda: {"seen_urls": ["https://x/a"], "total_saved": 1})
    monkeypatch.setattr(src, "_save_state", lambda state: None)
    monkeypatch.setattr(src, "_log", lambda msg: None)
    monkeypatch.setattr(src, "list_article_urls", lambda: iter(["https://x/a"]))
    called = []
    monkeypatch.setattr(src, "parse_article", lambda url: called.append(url))
    assert src.crawl(limit=5) == []
    assert called == []  # never re-parsed

def test_crawl_marks_non_article_as_seen_without_saving(monkeypatch):
    src = _Dummy()
    saved_states = []
    monkeypatch.setattr(src, "_load_state", lambda: {"seen_urls": [], "total_saved": 0})
    monkeypatch.setattr(src, "_save_state", saved_states.append)
    monkeypatch.setattr(src, "_log", lambda msg: None)
    monkeypatch.setattr(src, "list_article_urls", lambda: iter(["https://x/tag-page"]))
    monkeypatch.setattr(src, "parse_article", lambda url: None)  # not an article
    result = src.crawl(limit=5)
    assert result == []
    assert saved_states[-1]["seen_urls"] == ["https://x/tag-page"]

def test_crawl_continues_past_per_article_parse_exception(monkeypatch):
    src = _Dummy()
    monkeypatch.setattr(src, "_load_state", lambda: {"seen_urls": [], "total_saved": 0})
    monkeypatch.setattr(src, "_save_state", lambda state: None)
    monkeypatch.setattr(src, "_log", lambda msg: None)
    monkeypatch.setattr(src, "_append_article", lambda a: None)
    monkeypatch.setattr(src, "list_article_urls", lambda: iter(["https://x/bad", "https://x/good"]))
    def fake_parse(url):
        if url.endswith("bad"): raise RuntimeError("network blew up")
        from models import Article
        return Article(url=url, title="t", body="b", author=None, published_date=None,
                        category=None, source="dummy-test", scraped_at="now")
    monkeypatch.setattr(src, "parse_article", fake_parse)
    result = src.crawl(limit=5)
    assert [a.url for a in result] == ["https://x/good"]

def test_crawl_stops_at_limit(monkeypatch):
    # asserts last_stopped_reason == "limit reached" and crawl returns exactly `limit` articles
    ...

def test_crawl_discovery_exception_returns_partial_results_not_raise(monkeypatch):
    # list_article_urls() itself raises mid-generator -> outer except catches it,
    # state["last_stopped_reason"] starts with "error:", crawl() does NOT propagate
    ...
```

sections.title: providers.py
sections.content:
**`MadagascarTribune._is_article_link`** — pure predicate, 5 independent guard branches, zero tests. Highest ROI test in the whole file (cheap, catches real link-filtering regressions).

```python
from providers import MadagascarTribune

@pytest.mark.parametrize("href,expected", [
    ("article-slug.html", True),
    ("category-.html", False),          # rubrique convention, not an article
    ("https://external.com/x.html", False),
    ("/absolute-path.html", False),
    ("_AuthorName,123_.html", False),   # byline page
    ("no-extension", False),
    (None, False),
])
def test_is_article_link(href, expected):
    assert MadagascarTribune._is_article_link(href) is expected
```

**`MadagascarTribune._discover_categories`** — rubrique-id regex + sort-by-int (not lexicographic — `10` sorts after `9`), and the empty-list fallback on fetch failure.

```python
def test_discover_categories_sorts_numerically_not_lexically(monkeypatch):
    src = MadagascarTribune()
    fake_page = Mock(html_content='rubrique9 ... rubrique10 ... rubrique2')
    monkeypatch.setattr("providers.Fetcher.get", lambda *a, **k: fake_page)
    cats = src._discover_categories()
    assert cats == [
        "https://madagascar-tribune.com/spip.php?rubrique2",
        "https://madagascar-tribune.com/spip.php?rubrique9",
        "https://madagascar-tribune.com/spip.php?rubrique10",
    ]

def test_discover_categories_returns_empty_on_fetch_failure(monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr("providers.Fetcher.get", Mock(side_effect=RuntimeError))
    assert MadagascarTribune()._discover_categories() == []
```

**`FreeNews.list_article_urls`** — WP REST pagination: HTTP 400 (`rest_post_invalid_page_number`) stops cleanly, any *other* `HTTPError` re-raises (currently untested — a 500 or 403 should surface, not be swallowed), empty `posts` list also stops.

```python
import urllib.error
from providers import FreeNews

def test_freenews_pagination_stops_on_400(monkeypatch):
    calls = {"n": 0}
    def fake_urlopen(req, timeout):
        calls["n"] += 1
        if calls["n"] == 1:
            return _json_response([{"link": "https://x/1"}])
        raise urllib.error.HTTPError("url", 400, "bad", {}, None)
    monkeypatch.setattr("providers.urllib.request.urlopen", fake_urlopen)
    assert list(FreeNews().list_article_urls()) == ["https://x/1"]

def test_freenews_reraises_non_400_http_error(monkeypatch):
    def fake_urlopen(req, timeout):
        raise urllib.error.HTTPError("url", 500, "server error", {}, None)
    monkeypatch.setattr("providers.urllib.request.urlopen", fake_urlopen)
    with pytest.raises(urllib.error.HTTPError):
        list(FreeNews().list_article_urls())
```

**`Moov.list_article_urls`** — BFS frontier dedup (`queued` set prevents re-visiting), `/article/` filter excludes unrelated links.

```python
def test_moov_bfs_dedupes_and_filters_non_article_links(monkeypatch):
    home = Mock()
    home.css = lambda sel: [Mock(attrib={"href": "/article/a"}), Mock(attrib={"href": "/other/x"})]
    page_a = Mock()
    page_a.css = lambda sel: [Mock(attrib={"href": "/article/a"})]  # self-link, must not requeue
    responses = {"https://new.moov.mg/": home, "https://new.moov.mg/article/a": page_a}
    monkeypatch.setattr("providers.Fetcher.get", lambda url, **k: responses[url])
    urls = list(Moov().list_article_urls())
    assert urls == ["https://new.moov.mg/article/a"]
```

**`PROVIDERS` registry** — a cheap smoke test that every entry is internally consistent (dict key matches the class's own `source_name`), which would catch a copy-paste typo when adding a provider.

```python
from providers import PROVIDERS

def test_provider_registry_keys_match_source_name():
    for key, cls in PROVIDERS.items():
        assert cls.source_name == key

def test_every_provider_has_a_url_discovery_strategy():
    for cls in PROVIDERS.values():
        inst = cls()
        assert inst.sitemap_url or type(inst).list_article_urls is not cls.__mro__[1].list_article_urls
```

sections.title: Integration gaps (no fixtures currently exist)
sections.content:
- No test parses a **real saved HTML fixture** per provider against its `SiteSelectors` — the only thing that currently catches a selector going stale (site redesign) is a production run failing silently and `parse_article` returning `None`. Worth one `tests/fixtures/<provider>.html` + assertion per provider, at least for the two hardest ones (`madagascar-tribune`'s custom 3-step crawl, `midi-madagasikara`'s stealth path).
- No test on `main.py`'s CLI wiring (`--limit 0` = unlimited, `provider all` iterates every registered provider, `on_save` callback fires with `flush=True` for the TUI).
- No test that `Article`'s mutable default (`images: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)`) doesn't leak state across instances — cheap one-liner, currently unverified:

```python
from models import Article

def test_article_images_default_not_shared_between_instances():
    a = Article(url="1", title="t", body="b", author=None, published_date=None, category=None, source="s", scraped_at="now")
    b = Article(url="2", title="t", body="b", author=None, published_date=None, category=None, source="s", scraped_at="now")
    a.images.append("x")
    assert b.images == []
```

---
Skipped: `apps/cli` (Go, untested too, but the user's message and recent diff both point at the crawler — say if you want the same pass on the TUI) and `apps/web`/`apps/intro`. Add when asked.

rawOutputPreview:
## Test coverage: `apps/crawler` (Python)

**Current state: zero tests.** `pytest` is a dev dependency (`pyproject.toml`) but no `tests/` dir exists, no test files anywhere. Every function below is currently only exercised by running the crawler against live sites.

### base.py — pure logic, cheap to test, currently 100% untested

**`parse_french_date`** / **`parse_numeric_date`** — regex + dict lookup, no try/except around `datetime(...)` construction (invalid day/month int like `32` or `13` raises uncaught `ValueError`).

```python
# tests/test_base.py
import pytest
from base import parse_french_date, parse_numeric_date

@pytest.mark.parametrize("text,expected", [
    ("22 juin 2019", "2019-06-22"),
    ("octobre 30, 2017", "2017-10-30"),
    ("1 février 2020", "2020-02-01"),   # accented month
    ("1 fevrier 2020", "2020-02-01"),   # unaccented month
    ("no date here", None),
    ("22 blorpuary 2019", None),        # unknown month name
])
def test_parse_french_date(text, expected):
    assert parse_french_date(text) == expected

def test_parse_numeric_date_with_trailing_time():
    assert parse_numeric_date("13/07/2026 10:30") == "2026-07-13"

def test_parse_numeric_date_invalid_no_match():
    assert parse_numeric_date("not a date") is None

def test_parse_french_date_invalid_day_raises():
    # ponytail-gap: no bounds check before datetime() — documents current (crashing) behavior
    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        parse_french_date("32 juin 2019")
```

**`discover_sitemap_urls`** — sitemapindex recursion, namespace-stripped `loc` matching, and the deliberate exclusion of nested `image:image/image:loc` are the load-bearing logic here; none of it is tested.
